claimText: The S-400 Triumf, also known as Sudarshan Chakra, is an air defence system acquired from Russia that provides 360-degree radar coverage and multi-missile compatibility.
evidenceExcerpt: S-400 Triumf (Sudarshan Chakra) acquired from Russia offers 360-degree radar and multi-missile compatibility.
examUse: Statement-based questions on defence acquisitions and their specific capabilities.
distractorHooks: Acquired from USA or Israel instead of Russia
Provides limited directional coverage instead of 360-degree

claimText: Barak 8 is a Medium/Long Range Surface-to-Air Missile (MR/LR SAM) jointly developed by India and Israel, available in both maritime and land variants.
evidenceExcerpt: Barak 8 is a joint India-Israel MR/LR SAM with maritime and land variants.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching missile systems with their partner countries or operational ranges.
distractorHooks: Jointly developed with Russia or France instead of Israel
Short-range missile instead of MR/LR SAM

claimText: The Akash Weapon System is an indigenous short-range Surface-to-Air Missile (SAM) that features built-in Electronic Counter-Counter Measures (ECCM).
evidenceExcerpt: Akash is an indigenous short-range SAM with built-in Electronic Counter-Counter Measures (ECCM).
examUse: Statement-based questions testing the range and specific electronic warfare capabilities of indigenous missiles.

claimText: Akashteer is a fully indigenous, AI-driven, automated Air Defence Control and Reporting System developed by Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL).
evidenceExcerpt: Akashteer is an indigenous, AI-driven, automated Air Defence Control and Reporting System developed by BEL.
examUse: Institution-feature questions or statement-based questions identifying the purpose and developer of defence projects.
distractorHooks: Developed by DRDO or HAL instead of BEL
It is a surface-to-air missile instead of a Control and Reporting System
